summary: Neisseria gonorrhoeae causes this presentation due to its ability to form intracellular Gram-negative diplococci seen in urine smears and grow on selective media like Thayer-Martin agar. Its capacity for antigenic variation leads to repeated infections post-treatment, contributing to persistent symptoms such as dysuria, purulent discharge, and fever episodes.
